steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion: 'Vascularity and other identifying characteristics of pneumatic diverticula tend not to fossilize.' Read what comes next: 'so paleontologists have relied on studies of living bird species.' The word 'so' signals that the underlined portion is the cause that prompted an alternative method.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'led paleontologists to question the applicability of studies of certain living species': inverts the logic; the passage says paleontologists used living-species studies, not questioned them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'accounts for disagreements among paleontologists': the passage describes no disagreement among researchers. ✗
  • Choice C — 'previously led paleontologists to misidentify certain characteristics': the passage says characteristics don't fossilize, not that they were misidentified. ✗
  • Choice D — 'indicates why paleontologists have used another approach in addition to fossil analysis': the underlined portion explains the limitation (characteristics don't fossilize) that caused paleontologists to add living-species studies. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
